Source_Species.reason: The paper explicitly studies wheat and repeatedly states 'wheat (Triticum aestivum) cv. Cadenza'.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicitly the transformed/applied species is 'Triticum aestivum'.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The transformed plants are cv. Cadenza.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The Results directly measure and discuss 'free asparagine' concentrations in grain/seeds/flour.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Asparagine is a free amino acid, and the paper analyses it within the free amino acid pool.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Summary states free asparagine is the precursor for acrylamide formed during baking, toasting and high-temperature processing of wheat-based foods.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Directly supported by Results: triple-null edited lines had large reductions in grain free asparagine; plant 59 had mean 0.14 mmol/kg in T2 seeds (9% of wildtype, i.e. >90% reduction), T3 means for lines 23 and 59 were 57% and 48% of wildtype, and A-genome-null line 178 showed a smaller reduction.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed finding is supported in Results/Summary: CRISPR editing of TaASN2 homeologues, strong reduction in free asparagine especially in line 59, partial reduction in A-genome-null line 178, increases in glutamine/glutamate/aspartate, and poor germination rescued by exogenous asparagine.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TaASN2 is described as an asparagine synthetase gene.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the supplied paper text.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper identifies TaASN2 as asparagine synthetase and discusses asparagine synthesis from glutamine and aspartate, supporting placement in asparagine biosynthesis/nitrogen amino acid metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The Results/Discussion describe the asparagine synthetase reaction using glutamine and aspartate, which supports ATP-dependent asparagine synthesis as the relevant subpathway.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states: 'The reaction catalysed by asparagine synthetase involves glutamine and aspartate as the donor and receptor, respectively, of an amino group, and glutamate as the other product in addition to asparagine.' This supports the stated reaction description.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Glutamine and aspartate are explicitly mentioned as substrates/donor-receptor in the paper, but ATP is not explicitly stated in the supplied text, so the full field value is only partially supported.

Primary_Product.reason: Asparagine and glutamate are supported by the paper, but AMP and PPi are not mentioned in the supplied text.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TaASN2 directly encodes asparagine synthetase, and free asparagine is the measured target metabolite reduced by editing.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Asparagine synthetase directly forms asparagine from precursors, so its role as the terminal step to the target product is directly inferable from the described reaction and measured product.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper strongly supports TaASN2 as a major determinant/key control for grain free asparagine, especially the seed-expressed isoform, but it does not explicitly call the reaction 'rate-limiting'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TaASN2 is an asparagine synthetase gene, and the Results explicitly state that the first exon encodes the glutamine amidotransferase domain. However the separate 'synthetase domain' is not detailed in the supplied text; overall the family/domain statement is largely supported.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The Discussion states TaASN2 'is the only one to be expressed seed-specifically' and the Results state the A-genome TaASN2 gene had been shown to be relatively highly expressed.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Applied species is the same wheat background; seed/grain-specific expression and relatively high A-genome expression are supported by the same statements.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Free asparagine was measured in grain, seeds, and wholemeal flour from grain.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All three parts are supported: acrylamide relevance during high-temperature processing is stated; probable heat stress in the glasshouse is discussed as increasing T3 free asparagine; exogenous 0.1 M asparagine rescued poor germination.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Poor germination is directly reported, increased glutamine/glutamate/aspartate are shown/discussed, field testing is noted as not yet done, and some lines showed increased fifty grain weight while line 59 did not.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Experimental procedures/Results: CRISPR/Cas9 editing, amplicon NGS genotyping, GC-MS and HPLC amino acid profiling, multi-generation segregation analysis, phenotyping, and asparagine rescue of germination.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper says 'EnsemblPlants database references for the genes are given in Data S1 (Sheet 2),' so the statement that references are in Data S1 is supported, but no specific accession number is present in the supplied text.

Omics_Data.reason: Amplicon NGS on Illumina MiSeq and metabolite profiling by GC-MS/HPLC are explicitly described; no public accession number is given in the supplied text.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The Results state the Cas9 gene was 'driven by a maize ubiquitin promoter'.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion explicitly frames TaASN2 editing as encouraging for development of low-asparagine commercial wheat varieties and notes stronger effect of triple-null edits, partial effect of A-genome nulls, feasibility of Cas9-free plants, and germination/environment considerations.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All components are supported: gRNAs were designed not to target TaASN1/TaASN3.1/TaASN3.2/TaASN4; editing continued into later generations; some low-asparagine plants were Cas9-free by T2; and the Discussion suggests other ASN genes may contribute under stress while TaASN2 remains a major determinant.

corrections.reason: No EC number appears in the supplied paper text.
